park_name: Broome's Gateway Pet Friendly Caravan Park

reviews.review_text: Lovely place! Showers and toilets always spotless! They were able to fit us in last minute a day early which we appreciated:)

Great place we are staying 3 nights very busy and full staff are very friendly and polite old amenities but clean and they work 😆 great price for powered site for 2 and our dog as it's pet friendly great.AND the best sunrise and sites ever.

All good except that they allow generators which spoiled our time there.
Go with the times and get rid of them! They are a thing of the past....

Fantastic spot 15 mins out of Broome. The toilets and showers were very clean and the park is quiet. Ride in with 4 mates on adventure motorbikes after some gruelling days in the saddle. Dave the owner really went out of his way to make sure we were sorted. Pub 5 mins up the road does amazing dinners. A great stay and will be back.
Pretty good, relaxed, large sites, clean ensuite amenities
A Great park, Nice clean facilities, very Helpful staff. Only 20 minutes out of Broome. Would recommend Broome Gateway

great place to stay - facilities were lovely. easily the best shower i had between perth and broome. only problem is the showers and toilets are a two for one deal so using the bathroom can be a wait during peak showering times. BUT the tranquil vibes here more than make up for it. would absolutely stay again.

Highly recommend staying here. Great location out of the busy Broome. Dog friendly. $40 per night, well worth every cent. Lovely clean amenities & super friendly, helpful staff.
Did I mention the big size  camp sites. Great place to stay . If you don’t like busy and loads of people, stay here.
Big thanks to Nick & the team for nice clean amenities & flat shady camp sites. Would highly recommend.
Great caravan park. Large drive thru sites. Friendly staff. Clean amenities. Water. What more do you need. Would come again.
Big sites|Plenty of fresh water|Great big clean shower/toilet rooms |Friendly staff|Location great|Laundry only $6|$40 per night|Have a great meal at Roebuck roadhouse

Stayed here with a 12 person group and were given a lovely open area to ourselves. Hosts are super nice and accomodating, we were very grateful for their hospitality.
The amenities are fantastic, nice hot showers, a laundry and even a spot to wash the car!
It was very good value and only 22min from Broome so really accessible.
They can manage your gas bottle refills too and we had access to fresh water to replenish our tanks.

Flat, easy access sites far enough off the highway for a quiet sleep. Most sites had good, clean water hookup, but no powered options.
The toilets and showers were excellent, clean and well maintained.
It is very pet friendly with many campers walking dogs around the park.
The access road is dirt, but flat and short
We had such an Awesome stay but it was the caretakers Nick and Aleeshia that were the tipping point to 5 stars for us. They weren't just efficient but so helpful when we had a bit of a problem.

amazing place to stay with clean and spacious amendments, friendly staff and great prices!
Would 100% recommend

Park C8 has a wopping big pole at the front (near) centre of this bay. You won't have full access to your park. Although it could be good as a hammock pole. Also, you'll pay $20 deposit for a swipe to accessamenities, they keep that for two weeks. Probably bad people did something and now good people are paying for it.
Love this little Park, 20 minutes out of Broome, nice and quiet with large sites!
A bit rough and very few toilets etc for such a large park but generous sites.

We thoroughly enjoyed our 3 nights here. Spacious clean shaded areas makes it feel like you’re bush camping with amenities.
Which, by the way, are superbly spacious and very clean. Yes it gets busy at peak times however there’s enough to use if you’re prepared to be patient. Of course self contained makes life easier.
Water is available to fill up you can etc on leaving. Some sites have water available though it’s best to book if you want one.
Thanks to the friendly and helpful manager and staff who make sure everything runs smoothly.
Highly recommend this CP.
Sheryl & Shaun Reynolds
Liked the openess and country feel about it all. It is on a farm like. Based about 30 ks out of Broome.

Awesome stay with wonderful ammenities. Fantastic rural/ station atmosphere. Can’t say enough about how amazing the caretakers Alysia and Nik were. They made us feel so welcome and were amazing sorting out a problem for us.
The place is quiet and inexpensive. I would like to thank the French girl who works at reception. She was a great help. I recommend it!
A great, spacious campground & caravan park with excellent facilities, communal fire and helpful friendly staff.

Beautiful bathrooms, wonderfully clean and spacious. Hot water.
Caravan/camping bays very generous in size.

Fantastic base to explore Broome and surrounds, only 20 mins from town without the price tag. Super friendly and accomodating staff! Have stayed here twice now and cannot fault it. Huge bathrooms with good hot water and pressure, massive sites, doesn’t have that crammed in caravan park feel at all. Thankyou Alicia and co we will be back again :)

The new caretakers here are doing an amazing job - this is soooo. clean, sites are large, signage reflects organisation.  It is about 20 minutes out of town by car but so peaceful and quiet and no safety issues.  They even have an area to wash your van and car down.
Friendly, clean and peaceful

Beautiful shady campground with nice bathrooms (toilet, sink and shower in one room). Sites are spacious and they have drive through as well as normal sites. Dog friendly and the have an enclosed off leash area which was loved by our dog.
Staff was very friendly and helpful.

Great place and a short distance out of Broome. Unhooked the car and drove into town to do some necessary shopping. With Broome booked out we were pleasantly surprised and grateful to find this park. No power but water was plentiful and the shower block was amazing.

This caravan park has water to every site, but not power. The sites are large and easy to get onto. The amenities were great, unisex with large showers behind toilet wall. The bbq's were clean and worked well. There are lots of shady trees to park under and is only 40km from Broome

Half the cost of the price gougers in town.  BYO power but water provided and we loved the big toilet /shower combo blocks. Less busy than when we were here  4 weeks ago and kudos to staff who space campers out nicely.
We extended our stay twice without an issue, staff were excellent.
Don't like using the generator for other guests sake, but it was warm and operators say OK 8am-8pm.
Highly recommended

Stayed here in June, we were charged $45 for an unpowered site and we were happy to pay that fee until we saw the state of the park. All the washing machines except one were out of order and the one that did work was charging $6 a load. The camp kitchen consists of two picnic tables, a fridge, sink, and was infested with ants (due to food not being cleaned off the sink area). The site that we had was on dirt with no shade whatsoever. For this price you’re better off going to a caravan park that is in Broome, will charge you the same rate and is not a 30 minute drive out of town.
Everything you need, nothing you don't, walk to town, super friendly & helpful team, amenities immaculate & they do dog-sitting: in their air-conditioned office no less👍👌

I stayed here for three nights, and it was disappointing. With only six unisex
shower cubicles, each combined with a toilet, the wait times during peak hours (5-9pm) were unbearable. If you need the toilet during these hours, you might as well drive the 30km into town because it will be quicker. The park is dusty with not a single patch of grass, making it an uncomfortable and unappealing environment. Only come here as a last resort. On the positive side, the staff were friendly, and its location out of town helps avoid peak tourist season congestion.
Great place to stay over night with water no power but at $40 per van very expensive.  Some of the areas need a good clean up.

If you don’t mind standing out of Broome then this place is great. The bathrooms are clean and tidy, plenty of washing machines (no dryers though) and the BBQ’s and kitchen facilities are fine. There is no kettle or toaster in the camp kitchen though- and no power points to charge your devices or use your blow dryer etc. overall we enjoyed our stay here. Just be prepared for the red sand !
Heaps of room between sites

We can't recommend this place highly enough. Dave the owner went out his way to accomodate us and store our van when we had to fly overseas to attend a family funeral recently.
Being 20mins out of town was not an issue as we actually prefer having a bit more room that a bush setting offers rather than being jam-packed into a busy caravan park.

$40 night for red dirt, no power and no dump point, probably the worst value for service we've seen, they did have heaps of washing machines at $6 load! Must be big revenue from that but not enough for a dump point!
Dobra lokalizacja.Dobra cena.Czyste toalety.Brak prądu.
Nice set up, got us out of a sticky situation with overnight.

Nice open camp site, but not family friendly. They charge $40 per night for 2 adults then an extra $20 per night for kids over 12. This made it the most expensive campground we've stayed at for 2 adults and 2 teenage kids. $80 per night for no power and no dump point is a bit over the top.

Great dog friendly caravan park to stay at within a 20min short drive into Broome. Very friendly & accommodating staff. Large sites, bathroom/shower facilities which are immaculately clean and no restrictions with drinking water. Pet sitting service offered. Large fire pit area to socialise. No power but you are able to use generators 8am - 8pm. Definately worth a visit 👌

What a gem gateway is! On arrival you are welcomed with big smiles from the reception, the sites are large and well maintained, and the facilities are clean. This place has a beautiful and peaceful atmosphere and we will be returning in the future.
Spend your money somewhere else. Absolute robbery this place, wish I booked the RAC for an extra $20
Expensive to park on a piece of dirt. No power. Quite a way out of Broome.

This caravan park doesn't have powered sites. We stayed in the overflow area which was just scrub. Paying $36 a night to park on a piece of dirt. Only stayed there as all other caravan parks were full.
Clean, friendly, large sites, plenty of room and great price ( $36.32 non powered site ) for WA caravan parks which are mostly over priced on non powered sites

There was a lot to like about this caravan park. The check-in was easy, the staff were great, the sites were large with easy parking, there were many rubbish bins, and the water pressure was excellent. Unfortunately, it is an inconvenient distance from Broome if you're planning to stay more than a couple of nights, and the absence of a dump point seemed really odd for a park of this size. It's also well and truly time for a grader to smooth out the very bumpy roads through the park. Be aware that given all sites are unpowered, you'll be surrounded by generator noise from quite early until about 7 pm.

Caravan park
Plenty of space your not on top of each other. No power just water.

Not bad at all.  Found this place by chance & certainly not disappointed. Lots of room,..probably no need to book!  & large sites all on dirt. Water to tap into ,no power, showers ,toilets & laundry. Some shade also depending on site.
Downside would be 30km distance from town & men's toilets,...water from shower area comes into cubicles.  Facilities  dated but acceptable.

We have just spent a week here with our kids and puppy. Staff were very accomodating on arrival when we asked for a site away from others. Very spacious sites.Showers and toilets were clean and amazing hot water. We will definitely return when in Broome again 😀
Stayed the night here. Big and open.
Thanks for the overnight stay

Great staff, great amenities, good size sites, but we have a very inconsiderate neighbour. We are generator users ourselves, so not against people using them,but his has been on since they arrived and still on. Very noisy, exhaust facing us. We politely asked him if he could shift it a little. His reply was I can put it where I like. But very happy with the park.

Absolutely wonderful place, large sites, shady trees, great toilets/showers, plenty of washing machines and line space, lovely, friendly staff and it was beautiful waking up to the sound of birds in the morning.
We booked for two nights, extended another two and eventually stayed a week.
Highly recommend, our dogs were happy, will definitely stay again.
Great park 30 min from Broome very dog friendly even has an off leash pen for dog's
Great location, not far from Broome but without the broom prices. Great welcome from staff

Sites very dry and we out in back blocks and needed genny, no water had to fill container and carry. Toilet and laundry very clean. A drive to go into Broome for tours, trouble finding suitable park in Broome overnight! NO DUMP STATION!!!

Given it was booked out everywhere in Broome, this was our only choice.
Very large park, big sites, water supplied, but no power, generators allowed, amenities were adequate & clean, fees were good, staff were friendly, helpful.
20 kilometres out of Broome, but you can plan around it.
Packed like sardines We were given a spot for our caravan amongst the permanent residents .Very small sites are a challenge to parked Tradies grinding concrete next to us dusty & noise .
Excellent camp spot with water and reasonable unpowered sites and they are huge sites. Great set up and amenities. Woyld recommend.

Only place we could get in. Very nature based, terrible road in, so bumpy. Stayed 5 nights so has to drive 20 mins into broome every day but we just planned well. Need double the toilets there are, the ensuite bathrooms are great, add more of them! We were so far we had to drive. Ants everywhere near our site (overflow area) unfortunately. Frozen Mango amd eggs at the front desk was nice, washing expensive once again. Reasonable price per night compared to everywhere else in Broome/cable beach - so if you’re after affordable go here!!

We have been unable to move for 3weeks due to the closure of the Fitzroy Crossing the owners and their staff have been very accommodating the site and amenities have been kept clean and supplied we can highly recommend this park it doesn’t have all the bells and whistles but the friendly staff out weigh this
Friendly helpful staff. Good sized sites. It would be great if there was a separate area for people using generators. We were surrounded by them. Not sure why they ran them all day 8am - 8pm ???

1 night stay cost us $57. No powered sites. Supposedly water available. However only two taps to service 4 sites minimum. No tap splitters so we had no water either. Way too costly for no services. Ammenities in centre of the park so quite a trudge when you on the outskirts. Convenient location and not very busy considering everywhere else is booked out.
Love this bush style camping with just enough conveniences to make it very comfortable. Huge sites and very friendly staff. We'll be back.
Large area, a lot of van's, clean toilets and showers, well worth a visit
It's a non-powered caravan park. The sites are spacious, you can connect to water, the rates are reasonable, the showers are 6/5. I'll be staying here again

Excellent large park, lot sizes huge. No powered sites but generators allowed.
Vey good clean amenities.

beautiful caravan park !
it is a little out of town, but if you prefer a quiet space, this is the place to be.
the sites are huge, way bigger than on any caravan park I have been before. There are water taps with drinking water all over the park. The facilities are big and clean. And the staff is really kind and helpful.
Would recommend this park to everyone and I will definitely come back whenever I visit Broome.
stay safe ! ♡

Hard to rate. It has its good and bad parts.

Pros:
- It's not too far from town so if you want somewhere a little quieter it's good
- Seems to have a fairly large amount of sites
- Really good sized dog run
- toilets are ensuites with showers so it's a nice luxury
- showers are a brilliant size with great temp and water pressure

Cons:
- Doesn't feel like enough toilets for the amount of sites, i think you'd forever be lining up if you were here during a busy period
- Our site and the ones surrounding were absolutely infested with so many different types of ants. Also heaps of ants around the amenities blocks as well
- No powered sites at all, but they encourage generators. We're very glad no one was using a generator while we were there
- Some sites have an abundance of shade, most have barely any at all
The refrigerator is not working properly as the seal is broken. The washing machine only has cold wash. I didn’t find the staff helpful when I had a problem with my caravan.
